description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden or fabric-covered frame, featuring two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked one above the other and connected by struts and wires. The fuselage is slender and tapers toward the tail, with a single open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing. The nose houses a single piston engine with a two-bladed propeller, and the undercarriage consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, characteristic of early taildragger designs. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and rudder, along with horizontal stabilizers. The wings and tail surfaces are marked with roundels, suggesting a military or historical context. The overall finish is a tan or light brown color, with visible panel lines and structural details.
description: A vintage tan biplane with dual stacked wings, open cockpit, and a single front-mounted propeller engine. Features include a taildragger landing gear and roundel markings on the wings and tail.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the tail structure, vertical stabilizer, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Side-top view highlighting the stacked biplane wings, roundel markings, and the propeller at the nose.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical arrangement of the biplane wings.
description_view_3: Side-top view from the opposite angle, again showing the dual wings, cockpit, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
